rag_rules: Insert generated AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model for AGREE expressions
Preserve existing subcomponents and connections when adding implementation-level bindings
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE expressions
Place assumptions and guarantees in the appropriate scope based on the component type or implementation context
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
